LIMS Developer | Laboratory Systems Modernisation

Are you a Developer with a passion for scientific data systems? We are seeking a talented LIMS Software Engineer to join a major multi-site modernisation project. You will play a key role in simplifying laboratory workflows, developing custom functionality, and ensuring high-quality data management for a leading scientific organisation.

This is a collaborative role working alongside Subject Matter Experts and Senior Developers to deliver a unified, efficient, and secure LIMS solution.

The Role

Reporting to the Senior Developer, you will be responsible for:

Coding & Configuration: Developing new macros and custom functionality using proprietary LIMS scripting languages.

Solution Design: Participating in requirements gathering workshops to translate business needs into technical specifications.

Testing & QA: Leading unit testing, bug fixing, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities, including scenario mapping and script preparation.

Documentation: Producing high-quality technical documentation and maintaining best-practice software engineering standards.

Support & Training: Providing technical input for end-user training and assisting LIMS Administrators with BAU support and system enhancements.

About You

To be successful in this role, you will bring a blend of technical expertise and an understanding of laboratory environments.

Requirements:

Qualifications: Tertiary degree in Computer Science, Science, or a related field.

LIMS Expertise: Proven experience with commercial LIMS systems or large-scale computer-based data management systems.

Technical Skills: Strong coding/scripting abilities and experience in the full software development lifecycle (SDLC).

Laboratory Context: Experience working within a scientific analytical laboratory is highly regarded.

Soft Skills: A self-motivated approach with the ability to exercise sound judgement in a fast-paced, changing environment.

Baseline security clearance preferred.
Why Apply?

Impact: Contribute to a high-profile project that improves process efficiency and service delivery across multiple scientific branches.

Culture: Join a team that values diverse perspectives, professional development, and a strong culture of safety.

Collaboration: Work in a hybrid environment alongside experts in forensic, environmental, and biological sciences.

Interested in modernising the future of laboratory informatics? Click APPLY to submit your resume.

· $150,000 guideline + Bonus + Equity share

· Self-directed role

· CBD location

· Hybrid work environment

Our client, SPARC AI. https://sparcai.co is an Australian founded, publicly listed technology company, operating across Australia, The United States and Canada. Built from the ground up by a team of engineers, SPARC AI develops cutting-edge software and AI products for the defence, robotics and drone industries – systems that are designed to be deployed on autonomous platforms worldwide.

The company has a bold mandate to build the AI and software that will power the next generation of drones, robotics and defence systems. The business is growing and now seeks to add to its AI development team by this strategic appointment.

The role:

· Lead the design, development and deployment of AI and software for drone and robotics platforms

· Product innovation from concept to customer launch

· Build data pipelines for training, evaluation and continuous model improvement

· Develop APIs and integrations bringing AI capabilities into real-world operational environments

· Run structured experiments to benchmark model performance reliability and latency

· Contribute to engineering best practice and help shape the technical culture of a growing team

The skillset:

· 3+ years of experience in machine learning, applied AI, or a closely related field

· Advanced proficiency required in TypeScript, React, PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM, and GeoJSON/geospatial mathematics

· Hands on experience with LLM APIs and prompt engineering

· Comfortable working across the ML stack, data, training, serving and evaluation

· Experience deploying and monitoring models in cloud environments

· Self-directed, and comfortable with ambiguity who can set their own priorities

· Strong communication skills

The Person:

You will be an enthusiastic true AI believer who is always thinking about AI and how best to deploy it; you are the engineer who is actively experimenting with AI driven automation in their own work and who constantly asks how AI can improve the flow when they see a manual process. Your enthusiasm infects the team and allows it, and you, to build scalable business propositions round the AI model because you know they can be better and your AI input is making them better.

The reward:

· Designed to attract highflyers

· Equity opportunity in a public company

· High ownership, direct product impact, no layers of bureaucracy, report to the CEO

· Huge career development opportunities with this international company

Company Description
Nine is Australia's largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you'll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country's most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands.
Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.
Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We're evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.


Data Engineering at Nine empowers individuals to own delivery end-to-end, and explore how new data and the latest Data & AI technologies can improve workflows, and deliver greater value for our stakeholders.

We have a great opportunity for a Data Engineer to join our Commercial Growth Squad. Your role will be instrumental in building and sustaining Nine's competitive advantage through data, as you'll focus on the full development and maintenance lifecycle of innovative data products. This critical work directly empowers our business to create unique, actionable insights, which are essential for driving strategy and maximising our ad revenue.

What you'll do:

Design and implement data models that align with business needs.

Develop and maintain reliable data pipelines (ETL) to load and transform data from various sources into data warehouses.

Ensure pipelines are scalable and efficient, considering factors like data volume, velocity, and variety.

Implement data quality checks and validation processes to maintain accurate data.

Set up monitoring and alerting to proactively identify and resolve issues.

Perform root cause analysis for incidents and implement corrective actions.

Contribute to the technical design and vision for the data platform.

Explore new AI technologies and methods to enhance value delivery across Data Engineering workflows.

What you'll bring:

Strong programming ability in at least one language (e.g., Python).

Proficiency in SQL.

Data Modeling experience, including the application of abstract concepts in an enterprise environment.

Proven experience designing and building data pipelines, ETL processes, and managing data infrastructure.

Experience delivering workflow orchestration solutions and managing task dependencies using a tool like Apache Airflow.

A strong analytical mindset, a passion for tackling complex technical challenges, and the ability to find creative solutions to issues.

Desirable:

A bachelor's or master's degree in Computer Science, Software/Data Engineering, Data Science, IT, or a related field.

Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), AWS, or Azure, including their data storage, processing, and analytics services.

Familiarity with cloud deployment and test automation using a CI/CD solution (e.g., Cloud Build, Dagger, or Concourse).

Experience in deploying cloud infrastructure as code (IaC) using Terraform or similar tools.

Experience leveraging AI to deliver Data Engineering solutions.

Additional Information

How we work
At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team.

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers

Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.

Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads

Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription

More info at Nine Careers

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Work rights: Please note to apply for this role you must already have the right to lawfully work and live in Australia.

Job Title: Cloud Technical Architect (3 x roles Perm/Fixed Term Contract(s) available
Ad Text:
Novigi is the data technology, and AI partner to the Financial Services Industry with offices in Sydney, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Wollongong and Colombo, Sri Lanka. At our core, we are data-driven transformation and business optimisation experts. We partner with our clients to transform their business functions through the clever use of data and interoperability. We do this by offering a range of services and solutions.  

Location:

Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, or Wollongong

About the Role:

We are seeking an experienced and motivated Cloud Technical Architect with a strong background in infrastructure architecture to support the transition of superannuation administration systems.

Key Responsibilities and Qualifications:

5+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure and technical architecture, with a strong emphasis on Microsoft Azure.
Demonstrated experience as a Cloud Technical Architect (or equivalent), taking high-level solution architectures and producing detailed low-level designs (LLD) for cloud infrastructure, platforms, and integrations, with deep expertise in Microsoft Azure.
Expert-level knowledge of Microsoft Azure services across IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS, including strong hands-on experience with networking, security, governance, identity & access management, and cloud security best practices.
Solid experience designing and implementing cloud migration strategies, hybrid cloud architectures, and microservices-based solutions in Azure.
Proven ability to translate high-level architectural patterns and business requirements into comprehensive low-level technical designs, including detailed component specifications, configuration standards, API/integration contracts, infrastructure-as-code modules, security controls, and operational runbooks.
Expertise in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Bicep, ARM) and CI/CD pipelines. Deep understanding of DevOps and Platform Engineering principles, with the ability to define actionable implementation patterns for engineering teams.
Highly skilled at producing clear, detailed technical documentation, including low-level design documents, sequence diagrams, data flow diagrams, Azure resource configurations, and implementation guides that can be directly actioned by engineers.
Provide input to architectural governance and design assurance across cloud infrastructure and platform solutions.
Support delivery teams through build, migration, and transition phases to ensure low level designs are implemented correctly.
Mentor engineers and contribute to reusable architecture patterns and standards.
Ensure all designs meet Novigi security, compliance, and operational requirements.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work closely with Solutions Architects, engineering teams, and senior stakeholders to ensure high-level designs are implemented correctly and consistently at the detailed level.
A consultative, technically rigorous mindset with the ability to operate in complex and ambiguous environments while maintaining focus on delivering precise, buildable cloud solutions.
Relevant cloud certifications strongly preferred (e.g., Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Azure DevOps Expert, or equivalent); must be deeply familiar with the Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) and Well-Architected Framework (WAF), and able to apply them at a detailed technical level.
Knowledge of financial services with a particular focus on superannuation highly favourable, but not essential.

Why our clients love Novigi:

Novigi moves quickly, thinks sharply, and delivers what’s been agreed. Clients tell us we’re easy to work with because we stay focused on outcomes, cut through complexity, and keep communication open and honest. Behind that simplicity sits a huge depth of industry expertise and serious technical capability, built from years of working across superannuation, data, integration, and complex platforms. Our culture of innovation, collaboration and banter shows up in how we work day to day: teams that slot in seamlessly, challenge constructively, adapt as needs shift, and stay committed to getting the right things done well.

Only those candidates chosen to proceed will be contacted.

Senior Data Scientist for Artificial Intelligence Team | Fixed Term Contract

Are you a technically strong Data Scientist who enjoys solving complex problems end-to-end from experimentation through to production?

This company are building advanced AI capability within their organisation and are looking for a Senior Data Scientist to help design, deploy, and operationalise cutting-edge machine learning solutions across the business.

This is not a research only role. This is real world AI in production.

The Opportunity

You'll work across the full breadth of modern data science, including:

Natural Language Processing (NLP) & LLM applications
Time-series forecasting & anomaly detection
Computer vision & speech technologies
IoT and sensor data modelling
Statistical modelling and advanced ML algorithms
Cloud-native deployment (Azure environment preferred)

You'll take ownership of projects from concept through to deployment, collaborating with Infrastructure and Operations teams to ensure solutions are robust, scalable, and production-ready.

What You'll Be Doing

Design, build and deploy ML/AI models in managed cloud environments
Develop NLP solutions (NER, sentiment analysis, text classification, topic modelling, clustering, document understanding)
Apply specialised modelling techniques including geospatial analytics and time-series forecasting
Productionise models using MLOps best practices (CI/CD for ML)
Work across SQL/NoSQL data stores and ETL/ELT workflows
Communicate insights and progress clearly with business stakeholders
Contribute to innovation and technical direction within the AI function

What We're Looking For

4+ years' experience developing and deploying data science or AI models
Strong grounding in statistics and machine learning algorithms
Experience working with NLP and/or LLM technologies
Proficiency in Python (NumPy, pandas, SciPy, scikit-learn)
Experience deploying ML solutions in Azure (or similar cloud platforms)
Data engineering experience
Practical exposure to MLOps and model productionisation
Strong stakeholder communication skills

Why Join?

End-to-end technical ownership
Work on advanced AI problems not just dashboards
Exposure to diverse modelling domains (NLP, CV, IoT, time-series)
Opportunity to help shape and grow AI capability
Collaborative, forward thinking technology environment

If you're passionate about applying first-principles thinking to real operational challenges and want to see your models deployed at scale, we'd love to hear from you.

About Us

With a focus on innovation and delivering better customer outcomes, there has never been a better time to work in the Tasmanian energy sector with Aurora Energy. Be a part of Tasmania’s energy future and make a strong contribution to the ongoing success of our business.

We work hard to create a workplace that is welcoming and flexible, while balancing the needs of our people, the business and our customers. Our ‘Better Fit’ Program offers hybrid working after a training and/or probationary period to support work-life balance.

Aurora Energy will invest in you with training and education, celebrate your successes through recognition and reward, and provide a range of employee benefits including an electricity discount, generous leave benefits, and support for your health and wellbeing. 

We believe that every employee has a role to play in contributing to a positive and supportive workplace culture. Our Aurora Way values have been created by our people, for our people. As an employee, you’ll use these values every day to guide your behaviours and actions. Together we’ll continue to make Aurora Energy a great place to work. 

This role sits within our Digital Transformation and Operations Business Unit. They are at the forefront of developing and rolling out new technologies, partnering with the business to solve complex problems, keeping Aurora Energy’s data and systems secure, and much more.   

They collaborate with stakeholders across Aurora Energy to understand problems and identify the best way forward. They take a team-based, people-centric approach. In their words: “There are lots of exciting things to do to support Aurora Energy’s digital transformation, and this breadth is what keeps it interesting.”  

Digital Transformation and Operations is made up of three parts. The AI Lead sits within the Digital Strategy team.

Our Digital Strategy team helps drive our digital transformation and explores opportunities with the business to improve and solve problems. They’re known for working closely with the business and asking a lot of curious questions to make sure that they have the information they need to make the solution fit for purpose. They look after solution architecture and design through to delivery and operation. 

Aurora Energy has a significant focus on investing in digital solutions to support improved outcomes for our people and customers. It is a great time to join the team and be part of this exciting transformation.  

About the Role

As AI Lead, you’ll help shape how Aurora Energy uses artificial intelligence as part of our digital transformation. You’ll work across the business to spot opportunities where AI can make a real difference and help bring those ideas to life in a safe, practical and people‑focused way.

You’ll partner closely with teams across Aurora Energy to unpack complex challenges, ask curious questions, and turn ideas into ethical, fit‑for‑purpose AI solutions. You’ll help guide our AI capability and ways of working, while staying hands‑on where it adds the most value. This is a great opportunity to help build Aurora’s AI maturity and support better outcomes for our people and customers.

About You

You’re curious, collaborative and understand how to use AI to solve meaningful problems. You like working closely with the business, getting to the heart of what’s needed, and turning ideas into practical outcomes.

You’ll have:

Experience working with AI and associated tools
Experience in engaging stakeholders and developing strong relationships
The ability to consult across different business functions to assist with their unique challenges
The ability to explain complex ideas in a clear and engaging way
